resolved_footnotes: Two swabs will be taken at Visits 1 and 4. One will be tested (if possible at the site, otherwise at the central laboratory) within 24 hours and vaccination will only proceed if it is NAAT-negative for SARS-CoV-2 genomes. The second will be sent to the central laboratory for potential later testing.

footnotes.text: The COVID-19 illness visit may be conducted as an in-person or telehealth visit. This visit should not be performed if protocol amendment 20 is approved and participants are informed of early study completion prior to potential COVID-19 illness onset.

Two swabs will be taken at Visits 1 and 4. One will be tested (if possible at the site, otherwise at the central laboratory) within 24 hours and vaccination will only proceed if it is NAAT-negative for SARS-CoV-2 genomes. The second will be sent to the central laboratory for potential later testing.
If protocol amendment 20 is approved and participants are informed of early study completion, e-diary device return should be arranged by the site.

The first 5 participants in in each group will be observed at the site for at least 4 hours after study intervention administration. Further vaccination will commence no sooner than 24 hours after the fifth participant received his or her vaccination.

procedures.raw_name: According to eligibility, ascertain willingness to receive BNT162b2 if originally received placebo; if willing, unblind the participant's study intervention assignment (if not already done), and move placebo recipients to the SoA in Section 1.3.3
Administer study intervention
Assess acute reactions for at least 30 minutes after study intervention administration
Assign participant number
Collect blood sample for immunogenicity assessmente
Collect e-diary or assist the participant to delete application
Collect nonstudy vaccine information
Collect prohibited medication use
Collection of COVID-19–related clinical and laboratory information (including local diagnosis)
Confirm eligibility
Confirm use of contraceptives (if appropriate)
Explain participant communication methods (including for e-diary completion), assist the participant with downloading the app, or issue provisioned device, if required
For participants who are HIV-positive, record latest CD4 count and HIV viral load
Measure height and weight
Measure temperature (body)
Obtain demography and medical history data
Obtain informed consent
Obtain nasal (midturbinate) swab
Obtain randomization number and study intervention allocation
Perform clinical assessmentd
Perform urine pregnancy test (if appropriate)

Review temporary delay criteria

procedures.raw_name: Administer study intervention
Assess acute reactions for at least 30 minutes after study intervention administration
Collect AEs and SAEs as appropriate
Collect blood sample for HLA typingc
Collect blood sample for PBMC isolationc
Collect blood sample for immunogenicity assessment
Collect e-diary or assist the participant to delete application
Collection of COVID-19–related clinical and laboratory information (including local diagnosis)
Confirm eligibility
Confirm participant originally received BNT162b2 at Dose 1 and Dose 2
Confirm use of contraceptives (if appropriate)
Obtain informed consent
Obtain nasal (midturbinate) swab(s)
Obtain randomization number and study intervention allocation using the IRT system
Perform urine pregnancy test (if appropriate)
Review ongoing reactogenicity e-diary symptoms and obtain stop dates
Review reactogenicity e-diary data (daily review is optimal during the active diary period)

procedures.raw_name:
ADDITIONAL DOSE ONLY FOR THOSE PARTICIPANTS WHO RECEIVED DOSE 3 AT VISIT 8a or VISIT 301;

Participants who decline to receive a fourth dose of BNT162b2 will continue to follow schedule of assessments per Section 1.3.1 or 1.3.4 as appropriate.

ADDITIONAL DOSE ONLY FOR THOSE PARTICIPANTS WHO RECEIVED DOSE 3 AT VISIT 8a or VISIT 301;

procedures.raw_name: Additional Dose Eligibility Note (Visits 601-603)
Additional Dose Eligibility Note (Visits 604-606)
Administer BNT162b2
Assess acute reactions for at least 30 minutes after study intervention administration
Collect AEs and SAEs as appropriated
Collect nonstudy vaccine informationd
Collect prohibited medication used
Collection of COVID-19–related clinical and laboratory information (including local diagnosis)
Confirm participant has received at least 3 (or 4) prior doses as part of study and not outside and is eligible to receive fourth (or fifth) dose of BNT162b2
Confirm use of contraceptives (if appropriate)
Contact the participant by telephone
For participants who are HIV-positive, record latest CD4 count and HIV viral loade
Obtain informed consent
Obtain nasal (midturbinate) swab
Obtain vaccine vial allocation via IRT
Perform urine pregnancy test (if appropriate)
Request the participant return the e-diary device or assist the participant to delete the application
Review and consider eligibility
Review temporary delay criteria
